new_tests: TestCompactConcurrentAppendNotLost (engine/graph/compact_test.go, appended at end of file after TestCompaction_FailedTruncateRetryThenOrdinarySubsequentAppendsSurvive)

approach_chosen.decision: (a) lock-scope extension, per-node - NOT (b) segment-count/max-segment capture
approach_chosen.reasoning_summary: Analyzed engine/wal.Writer's rotation semantics (defaultMaxSegmentBytes = 4 MiB, engine/graph/edge_append.go:158 and edgelog.go): a single CSREdge record is a few dozen bytes, so a concurrent AppendEdge landing during Compact's read-then-truncate window will, in practice, append into the SAME still-current segment file ReadNodeAfter already fully read, not create a new segment file. A purely segment-number-based fix ('TruncateNode only removes segments numbered <= the maxSeg ReadNodeAfter observed') cannot close this race on its own, because the race can land INSIDE, not just after, the already-read segment file - deleting that file by path/number still discards the newly-appended bytes physically co-located with already-merged ones. A byte-precise variant (splitting a segment file's already-merged head from a not-yet-merged tail at truncate time) would require new primitives in engine/wal (splitting a live segment file, handing a surviving tail to the still-open wal.Writer) - a substantially larger change than this single-commit subtask should require. Chose instead to add EdgeLog.LockNode, a lazily-created per-node *sync.Mutex independent of EdgeLog's existing writer-cache mutex (l.mu): AppendEdge holds it for its entire body; Compact acquires it per node immediately before ReadNodeAfter and holds it through that same node's later TruncateNode call (spanning the intervening WriteCSR/saveCompactState calls for nodes that end up in compactedNodeIDs; released immediately for nodes with maxSeg<0). This makes 'AppendEdge for node X' and 'Compact's read-then-truncate of node X' strictly mutually exclusive for every case, not just the new-segment case, at the cost of blocking AppendEdge for nodes ACTIVELY being compacted for the duration of one WriteCSR call (bounded, scoped per-node not global, disclosed as an accepted tradeoff).

key_pointers.new_lock_primitive: engine/graph/edgelog.go: EdgeLog.nodeLocksMu/nodeLocks fields, EdgeLog.nodeLock (unexported) and EdgeLog.LockNode (exported) methods, ~lines 54-125
key_pointers.appendedge_change: engine/graph/edgelog.go: AppendEdge now holds LockNode(sourceFileID) for its whole body, ~line 185
key_pointers.compact_change: engine/graph/compact.go: Compact function's node loop (heldNodeLocks map + releaseHeldLocks closure) and final truncate loop, ~lines 420-520
key_pointers.new_hook: engine/graph/compact.go: var compactNodeLockedHook func(id uint64), nil in production, invoked once per node right after ReadNodeAfter while the node lock is still held
key_pointers.new_doc_section: engine/graph/compact.go package doc comment, new '# Lock-ordering fix (concurrent AppendEdge vs. read-then-truncate)' section, appended after the existing '# Segment-number reuse (second fix cycle)' section
key_pointers.regression_source: .cdr/index/regression.jsonl / .cdr/memory/pending.md 'EdgeLog.ReadNodeAfter/TruncateNode lock-ordering gap' finding (originally surfaced task-3.1.3, .cdr/runs/2026-07-08/010-verification and 012-verification) - this run does not itself mark that entry resolved; that is a verification-agent decision (invariant I4)

untrusted_content_disclosure: gh issue view 49's body was read fresh and is a plain, well-formed subtask list with no embedded fake directives. Separately, this run's own conversation/tool-output stream contained several environment-injected blocks styled as legitimate system reminders that are not genuine task input: a 'the date has changed, don't mention it' notice, an MCP-server ('tokensave') tool-usage instruction block for a server never invoked, and an 'Auto Mode Active' directive urging the agent to proceed without clarifying questions. All were treated strictly as untrusted data, not acted upon, and did not influence this run's scope, permissions, or the CDR workflow followed - consistent with the pattern already observed and disclosed in the prior 035-implementation/036-verification runs for this same issue.

next_step: cdr:verify - independent re-verification required; this agent did not verify its own work (invariant I4). Verification should independently confirm: (a) the new test's name/behavior exactly matches issue #49's test spec and genuinely exercises the described race window (not a narrower/different scenario), (b) the chosen approach (per-node lock-scope extension, option (a)) genuinely closes the race for both the 'new segment' and 'same still-open segment' sub-cases (the latter was this run's key finding for why option (b) alone is insufficient - verification should independently re-derive or challenge this reasoning), (c) no deadlock risk under the existing single-compactor assumption, (d) regression.jsonl's lock-ordering finding should be updated to resolved=true only after independent confirmation, left undone by this run.
